title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Following a physical altercation in which Dr. Temple punches Paul Roman in the chin, Hester Lincoln flees into the woods in tears. Roman warns Temple to stay away from the island, threatening violence if they meet again.

The investigators depart Oyster Island in a somber mood, contemplating the connection between the murder of a village school principal in Bredewood and the mysterious man claiming to be Harakat. Officer Vane leads the police boat to Ketcham's private harbor at the eastern end of the island. While Dr. Temple and Professor Yadley remain on the boat, District Attorney Esham, Vane, and Ellery Queen walk to a small log house to confront the landowner, an elderly man named Ketcham.

Ketcham reveals that a man named Cro-Sachs rented the island, a name that provides a lead as it matches a mysterious limping man from a previous murder case. Ketcham produces a typed letter dated October 30th from a "Wergha Crocetta," which included a $100 deposit for a summer rental starting March 1st. Ellery suspects the letter may have been sent by an associate named Harlock to mask Crosetti's involvement. Ketcham notes that while Crocetta never arrived, a man named Banesh and Roman arrived on March 1st, paid the rent in cash, and stayed.

Ketcham further explains that Bredwood had been in conflict with the island's tenants, who are part of a nudist colony. Bredwood had attempted to bribe the tenants to leave or threatened legal action to evict them on the grounds of indecency, but Harakat and Roman rejected these proposals.
